perturbation.original_text: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be sent by first -class registered or recorded delivery post to the party being served at its address specified above or at such other address of which such party shall have given notice as aforesaid, and marked for the attention of that party's signatory of this Agreement. The date of service shall be deemed to be the day following the day on which the notice was transmitted or posted as the case m ay be.
perturbation.changed_text: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be sent by email to the party being served at its email address specified above or at such other email address of which such party shall have given notice as aforesaid, and marked for the attention of that party's signatory of this Agreement. The date of service shall be deemed to be two days following the day on which the notice was transmitted or posted as the case m ay be.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the method of sending notices from 'first-class registered or recorded delivery post' to 'email' and the date of service is extended by one day. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about how notices must be served for them to be valid. If notice is sent by post, then date of service will be deemed the day following, but if its sent via email it will be deemed as two days following.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All notices under this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be sent by email to the party being served at its email address specified above or at such other email address of which such party shall have given notice as aforesaid, and marked for the attention of that party's signatory of this Agreement. The date of service shall be deemed to be two days following the day on which the notice was transmitted or posted as the case m ay be.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction as it modifies the method of sending notices from first-class mail to email and the date of service from one day following transmission to two days following transmission. The contradicted text states that notices should be sent via email and the date of service is two days following the day of transmission. The contradiction lies in the original text specifying both first-class mail and email as acceptable methods, while the changed text only allows for email. Moreover, it specifies that if the notice is sent via post (as it states in the first half of the sentence), the date of service would be one day following, which contradicts the contradicted text of two days following. Therefore, the modification creates a clear and direct inconsistency within the document regarding the acceptable method for sending notices and the date of service.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement ca nnot be changed except by written agreement between the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement can be changed by verbal agreement between the parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification changes the requirement for amendments from 'written agreement' to 'verbal agreement'. This in-text contradiction introduces uncertainty about the valid method for changing the agreement. If the agreement states that changes must be written, but another section allows for verbal changes, disputes can arise over the enforceability of verbal modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire Agreement and understanding between the parties in respect of Confidential Information and supersedes all previous agreements, understandings and undertakings in such respect and all obligations implied by law to the extent that they conflict with the express provisions of this Agreement. This Agreement can be changed by verbal agreement between the parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by stating the agreement can be changed by verbal agreement. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text stated that the agreement can only be changed by written agreement, while the modified text states it can be changed by verbal agreement. This falls under the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type because different sections (in this case, the same section after modification) provide conflicting methods for changing the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS : (A) The parties, for their mu tual benefit, may have exchanged and wish further to exchange certain information (including but not limited to trade secrets and proprietary know -how) in order that each of them may evaluate such information for the purpose of determining their respective interest in establishing a business relationship between them.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS : (A) The parties, for their mu tual benefit, may have exchanged and wish further to exchange certain information (including but not limited to trade secrets) in order that each of them may evaluate such information for the purpose of determining their respective interest in establishing a business relationship between them.
perturbation.explanation: The modification omits 'proprietary know-how' from the definition of information exchanged. This creates an in-text contradiction if later clauses refer to or depend upon 'proprietary know-how' as being covered under this agreement. Some of the information that might be disclosed is now not defined as Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In this Agreement the following expressions shall have the following meanings: (a) 'Purpose' shall mean any discussions , negotiations and development work undertaken between or within the parties concerning or in connection with Switchdig ital and the Applicant entering into a carriage agreement for carriage of the Applicant’s radio service on the DAB multiplex operated by Switchdigital ; (b) 'Confidential Information' shall mean all information or data disclosed (whether in writing, orally or b y any other means) to one party by the other party or by a third party on behalf of the other party and shall include but not be limited to (A) any information ascertainable by the inspection or analysis of samples, (B) the information described in the Sch edule 1 hereto as having been disclosed prior to the date hereof and (C) any information relating to that party's business, operations, prices, processes, plans, intentions, product information, know - how, design rights, trade secrets, software, market oppo rtunities, customers and business affairs, but shall exclude any part of such disclosed information or data which: (i) is or becomes in the public domain in any way without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; or
perturbation.justification: The original text included 'proprietary know-how' in the definition of information exchanged, while the changed text omits it. The contradicted text defines 'Confidential Information' to include 'know-how'. By removing 'proprietary know-how' from the initial definition, the changed text creates a contradiction because it implies that 'proprietary know-how' may no longer be considered 'Confidential Information' under the agreement, conflicting with the later clause that explicitly includes 'know-how' within the scope of 'Confidential Information'.
